steps.title: Separate complete clauses
steps.content: The sentence contrasts two complete ideas: many of Euclid's works are lost, and his Elements is extant. A conjunctive adverb such as conversely cannot join two independent clauses with only a comma.

steps.title: Reject faulty punctuation
steps.content: Choice A creates an awkward mix of and with conversely. Choice B creates a comma splice between complete clauses. Choice D leaves conversely poorly integrated and does not cleanly separate the clauses. A period before Conversely separates the two sentences, and the comma after it correctly introduces the contrast. The answer is C.
